spec_match.rationale: Touches TCP from the chromestatus summary but skips cross-site — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, input); controls include "Fast"; "Medium"; "Slow" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6496757559197696 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.mode-btn:hover:not(.active)).

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on connection-queue-simulator
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for TCP Socket Pool Limit Randomization introduces cross-site but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on connection-queue-simulator
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring connection-queue-simulator back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.mode-btn:hover:not(.active)). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: connection-queue-simulator for TCP Socket Pool Limit Randomization has structural gaps: the rubric flags multiple fails (see open questions). Needs a pass to convert it from a placeholder into a real concept demo.
